arxiv_papers.title: Poverty traps are rare, but trappedness isn't
arxiv_papers.snippet: The persistence of poverty is not well explained by who is poor. We argue the relevant object of measurement is trappedness--expected escape time from deprivation--which varies systematically across institutional environments and is invisible to standard poverty indices. Using Markov chains estimate

arxiv_papers.title: Measuring the depth of multidimensional poverty with ordinal data
arxiv_papers.snippet: This paper proposes a positional poverty gap measure of multidimensional poverty within the Alkire-Foster counting framework.
